programmes.show_description: Dead End: DI Frost is called to the scene of a kidnapping where the driver and conductor of a Denton bus have been captured in a revenge plot planned by two grief-stricken victims.
The Prize: Classic sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Everyone wants to go when Mum wins a holiday for two to the Costa Brava.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Seen a Ghost: A dark family secret comes to light when a girl deliberately runs over a woman on a pedestrian crossing, and the result of an internal inquiry board is revealed.
Bad Blood: Detective drama series. A couple threatened with violence unless they move out of their cottage are accused of murdering the landowner.
The Indelible Stain: Lewis and Hathaway investigate when a controversial American academic is murdered after speaking at Oxford's Department of Criminology.
The Departed: Comedy-drama series. A visit for Martin to London is disturbed on the way back home by the death of a local sheep farmer whose widow starts to see visions of him.
Classic musical adventure film starring Dick Van Dyke and Sally Ann Howes, 1968. A down-on-his-luck inventor turns a broken-down Grand Prix car into a fun vehicle for his children.
Bawdy British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1973. The town council of a seaside resort are persuaded to hold a beauty contest with chaotic results.
British comedy film starring Jim Dale and Kenneth Williams, 1969. A doctor tries to make a fortune selling a weight-reducing potion he found working at a mission in the South Seas.
World War II drama starring John Thaw. A curmudgeonly widower living in a small village takes in a painfully shy evacuee from London and teaches him to read, write and draw.
Four Funerals and a Wedding: Barnaby uncovers a decades-old battle of the sexes when an ageing feminist and a misogynist vicar are murdered, but whose side is the killer on?
Tainted Fruit: Feature-length drama. DCI Barnaby uncovers a seedy world of adultery and deceit within an affluent rural community when the body of an arrogant local beauty is found.
The first episode of a suspenseful crime drama. A deaf woman's lipreading skills aid the police in surveilling a dangerous gang, but her bonding with a criminal risks everything.
Suspenseful crime drama. Alison's vital lipreading aids the detectives in uncovering the gang's plans, but she can only conceal her meeting with Liam from them for so long.
The New Telly: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a bus company. Stan and his family decide to buy a colour TV, but there is a problem with the disposal of their old set.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Reading Between The Lines: Comedy series about rival antique dealers. An estate agent with unseemly plans for Simon and Oliver's neighbourhood is brought to book by a lovelorn Ringo.
Olive's Divorce: Classic sitcom about a bus company. Stan takes Olive to the divorce courts as Arthur has now been gone for six months.
The Bells Are Ringing!: Classic sitcom. Mike finds his true love and announces that he is getting married, but things get complicated when Sally falls for Mike's girlfriend's father.
You're Never Too Old to Be Young: Classic '70s sitcom. Jean feels that her marriage to Sid has gone stale, but Sid is annoyed when she decides to spruce it up as a result.
Some Enchanted Evening: Classic '70s sitcom. Jean is sulking at Sid and Sally and Mike won't talk to him - when Sid finds out why, he decides to take himself off to the pub.
The Right Way to Travel: 70s sitcom. Mildred's dreams of a cheap holiday clash with George's political allegiances.
Comedy spin-off film sequel starring Harry H Corbett and Wilfrid Brambell, 1973. Albert must die when things get very bad at the Steptoes' Shepherd's Bush rag-and-bone business.
British comedy film starring Jim Dale and Kenneth Williams, 1969. A doctor tries to make a fortune selling a weight-reducing potion he found working at a mission in the South Seas.
Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1972. A gang plans to raid a maternity hospital in order to steal a gigantic stock of birth control pills.
Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Kenneth Williams, 1972. A package holiday on the island of Elsbels turns into a hilarious adventure rife with innuendo and gags.
Bawdy British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1974. 1750 - a newly-formed police force attempt to catch Dick Turpin, the most notorious of all highwaymen.

programmes.show_name: Carry On... Don't Lose Your Head
programmes.show_description: Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Jim Dale, 1967. Two English noblemen decide to rescue their French counterparts from the revolting masses and Madame Guillotine.
Dance with the Dead: When the body of a young man is found in a Second World War-era motor car full of exhaust fumes, it looks like suicide, but all is not as it seems...
The very first episode of the detective drama series. Newly-arrived DCI McDonald and DS Dodds try to identity a man shot dead in a mansion.
